response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and leadership potential, they fall short on critical minimum requirements. The role explicitly requires 8 years of C++ programming, but the resume shows only ~1-2 years of C++ experience from 10+ years ago, with recent work (last 5+ years) focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and full-stack product engineering. The candidate's infrastructure experience comes from microservices and backend systems rather than cloud compute platform architecture, and technical leadership in cloud/DevOps engineering is not clearly demonstrated.
strengths: Proven experience with large-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests (Dunnhumby microservices infrastructure)
Strong track record mentoring and teaching through workshops, code organization initiatives, and developer tooling contributions
Consistent emphasis on software craftsmanship, TDD, CI/CD practices, and engineering best practices across multiple roles
concerns: Insufficient C++ experience (only ~1-2 years, mostly 10+ years ago; role requires 8 years as minimum qualification)
Recent career trajectory diverges from cloud infrastructure focus, shifting toward full-stack product development in TypeScript/Node.js
Technical leadership experience not clearly demonstrated specifically in cloud computing or DevOps engineering context
